briefing.headline: Rig up a Flipping Jig — steady summer approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Flipping Jig is a strong choice for summer with clear water and stable conditions.

Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a compact mini flipping jig instead of full-size. You catch more fish and get more bites. With ever-increasing fishing pressure, a smaller jig is what it takes to get bit.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.color: blue craw jig with green pumpkin sapphire trailer
alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Don't soak a jig. The more flips you can get in, the more fish you get in front of, the more bites you will get. If they aren't going to eat on the first couple of hops, they can starve.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, hump, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.
Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

Sealock: When the flipping/pitching bite gets tough, switch to a wacky-rigged Senko on a spinning rod with 8-lb test — fish it in the same areas (under docks, laydowns, boulders, grass lines); hook through the middle, make a long cast, let fall on...

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
